scenes.title: Animated Curve
scenes.description: Tests the `animated_curve` element: a y=f(x) curve with optional `fill_regions` for shading areas. Use the Doc tab for full reference.
scenes.markdown:
# Animated Curve

`animated_curve` plots a function $y = f(x)$ over a range and optionally shades **fill regions** between the curve and one or more boundary expressions. All fields are live — driven by sliders, time $t$, or scene functions.

---

## Minimal example

```json
{
  "type": "animated_curve",
  "expr": "sin(x)",
  "range": [-3.14159, 3.14159],
  "color": "#4da6ff"
}
```

---

## Full field reference

| Field | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `expr` | `"0"` | Expression for $y = f(x)$. Variable `x` is injected automatically. |
| `range` | `[-1, 1]` | `[xMin, xMax]` — numbers or expression strings driven by sliders / $t$ |
| `samples` | `200` | Number of sample points along $x$ |
| `color` | `"#ff8844"` | Curve line color |
| `width` | `3` | Line width in pixels |
| `opacity` | `1` | Curve opacity — number or expression string |
| `showCurve` | `true` | Set `false` to hide the line and show fills only |
| `fill_regions` | `[]` | List of fill region objects (see below) |

---

## `fill_regions`

Each entry in `fill_regions` shades an area relative to the curve.

```json
"fill_regions": [
  {
    "id": "my-fill",
    "color": "#4488ff",
    "opacity": 0.4,
    "above": "0",
    "rightOf": "a",
    "leftOf": "b"
  }
]
```

### Fill region fields

| Field | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | — | Optional identifier |
| `color` | inherits curve color | Fill color |
| `opacity` | `0.35` | Fill opacity — number or expression string |
| `above` | — | Fill where curve $\geq g(x)$. $g(x)$ is the lower boundary; fill spans $[g(x), \text{curve}]$ |
| `below` | — | Fill where curve $\leq h(x)$. $h(x)$ is the upper boundary; fill spans $[\text{curve}, h(x)]$ |
| `rightOf` | — | Fill where $x \geq$ this value (expression). Equivalent to a left x-boundary. |
| `leftOf` | — | Fill where $x \leq$ this value (expression). Equivalent to a right x-boundary. |
| `outlineWidth` | — | Width (px) of a line drawn along the boundary expression (`above`/`below`). Number or expression string. **Omit for no boundary.** |
| `outlineColor` | inherits `color` | Boundary line color |
| `outlineOpacity` | `min(1, opacity × 2)` | Boundary line opacity — number or expression string |

### Boundary semantics

| Boundaries specified | Region filled |
|---|---|
| `above` only | Between $g(x)$ and curve, where curve $\geq g(x)$ |
| `below` only | Between curve and $h(x)$, where curve $\leq h(x)$ |
| Both `above` and `below` | **Band fill** — directly from $g(x)$ (lower) to $h(x)$ (upper), where $g(x) < h(x)$. The plotted curve is not used as a boundary — both boundaries are supplied explicitly. Use `max`/`min` composition to combine a curve with a horizontal line: `above: "max(f(x), bot)"`, `below: "min(g(x), top)"`. |
| Neither | Between $y=0$ and curve (above and below the axis) |

When only `above` or `below` is set, the plotted curve is one boundary and the expression is the other. When **both** are set, the fill is a **band** between the two expressions — the curve is not used as a boundary at all.

`above` and `below` may depend on `x` (e.g. `"cos(x) - 1"`), enabling fill between two arbitrary curves.

When `outlineWidth` is set, a MathBox line is drawn along the full perimeter of the fill region. **Default: no outline.**

---

## Recipes

### Fill between curve and x-axis

```json
"fill_regions": [
  { "color": "#4488ff", "opacity": 0.4, "above": "0" },
  { "color": "#ff4455", "opacity": 0.4, "below": "0" }
]
```

Blue above zero, red below — two regions, each specifying one boundary.

### Definite integral shading

```json
"fill_regions": [
  { "color": "#88ffcc", "opacity": "fill_op", "rightOf": "a", "leftOf": "b" }
]
```

Sliders `a` and `b` control the integration window. No `above`/`below` → fills between curve and $y=0$.

### Fill between two curves (one-sided)

```json
{
  "expr": "sin(x)",
  "fill_regions": [
    { "color": "#aa88ff", "opacity": 0.4, "above": "cos(x) - 1" }
  ]
}
```

Shades where $\sin x \geq \cos x - 1$. The `above` expression is evaluated at each sample $x$.

### Band fill — all four constraints

```json
{
  "expr": "sin(x)",
  "fill_regions": [
    {
      "color": "#66aaff", "opacity": 0.45,
      "above": "max(cos(x) - C, bot)",
      "below": "min(sin(x), top)",
      "rightOf": "L", "leftOf": "R"
    }
  ]
}
```

Fills the band from the floor-clamped cos curve to the ceiling-clamped sin curve, only within $[L, R]$. The fill appears only where all constraints hold simultaneously. Use `max`/`min` composition to combine a curve boundary with a horizontal line as the active constraint whichever is tighter.

### Animated range

```json
{
  "expr": "sin(x)",
  "range": ["-pi", "pi + A * t"],
  "fill_regions": [{ "above": "0" }]
}
```

Both range endpoints are live expressions — the curve extends in real time.

---

## Notes

- `expr`, `above`, `below`, `rightOf`, `leftOf`, and `opacity` all support **slider variables**, **time `t`**, and **scene functions**.
- `above`/`below` boundaries support `x` as a variable, making fill-between-curves natural.
- All four membership conditions (`above`, `below`, `rightOf`, `leftOf`) are consistent: each names the region the fill occupies, not where it clips.
- For static curves that never change, `parametric_curve` with `y`/`x` fields is simpler. Use `animated_curve` when interactivity or fill regions are needed.

steps.title: Basic curve — no fill
steps.description: A simple `animated_curve` plotting `y = A·sin(x + φ)`. No `fill_regions` — just the curve line. Drag the sliders to reshape the wave.

steps.title: Fill between curve and x-axis
steps.description: Two fill regions on the same curve. The blue region uses `above: "0"` — fill where curve ≥ 0, spanning [0, curve]. The red region uses `below: "0"` — fill where curve ≤ 0, spanning [curve, 0]. No `rightOf`/`leftOf` — both cover the full x range.

steps.title: Fill clipped by left/right bounds
steps.description: One fill region using `rightOf: "L"` and `leftOf: "R"` — fill only where L ≤ x ≤ R. No `above`/`below`, so the fill spans from the curve to y=0. The outline traces the full perimeter: curve top, vertical caps at L and R, and the x-axis along the bottom.

steps.title: Fill between two curves
steps.description: The blue curve (`A·sin(x+φ)`) has a fill region with `above: "B·cos(x+ψ)−C"`. Because `above` accepts x-dependent expressions, it fills exactly where sin ≥ cos−C, with the cos curve as the lower boundary. Drag sliders to reshape both curves and watch the shaded area update.

steps.title: Above and below — both regions at once
steps.description: Same two curves, now with two fill regions on the sin curve. `above: "B·cos(x+ψ)−C"` (blue) fills where sin is on top; `below: "B·cos(x+ψ)−C"` (red) fills where cos is on top. Together they shade the complete area between the curves in two colors.

steps.title: Vertical boundaries with rightOf / leftOf
steps.description: Both fill regions now also use `rightOf: "L"` and `leftOf: "R"` — the fills only appear between x=L and x=R. Two `animated_line` elements mark the boundary positions as dashed vertical lines that move with the sliders.

steps.title: Band fill — all four constraints
steps.description: Two complementary band fills, all four constraints active in each. **Blue**: `above: max(cos, bot)` → `below: min(sin, top)` — fills where sin is on top, clamped to `[bot, top]`. **Red**: `above: max(sin, bot)` → `below: min(cos, top)` — fills where cos is on top, clamped to `[bot, top]`. Together they tile the entire `[bot, top]` band within `[L, R]`, split wherever the two curves cross.
